title: Fix payload_to_term Box::leak memory leak
purpose: payload_to_term uses Box::leak to keep binary term memory alive, leaking memory on every workflow start. Fix this to use proper memory management — either beamr's spawn argument copying, thread-local heap parking (like engine_nifs.rs), or a beamr API that accepts owned binary data.
task: Replace the Box::leak pattern in payload_to_term (crates/aion/src/runtime/payload.rs) with a non-leaking alternative that keeps the binary term valid through the spawn call. Verify with repeated workflow starts that memory does not grow unboundedly.

requirements.title: No memory leak on payload conversion
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT use Box::leak or std::mem::forget to keep payload binary terms alive. The heap allocation backing the binary term SHALL be freed after the beamr scheduler copies the spawn arguments onto the new process heap.
acceptance: payload_to_term does not call Box::leak or std::mem::forget.
Starting 1000 workflows in a loop does not cause monotonically growing RSS.
The binary term argument is valid at the time scheduler.spawn() reads it.

requirements.title: E2E activity dispatch still works
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL preserve the full E2E activity dispatch path: Gleam workflow receives the input binary, calls the NIF, worker receives the task.
acceptance: hello-world E2E test passes: server starts, worker connects, workflow starts, NIF fires, worker receives task.

boundaries: SHALL NOT change the Term type or beamr's spawn API — work within the existing interface.
SHALL NOT add unsafe code to the aion crate.
verification: No Box::leak or mem::forget in payload.rs.
